# Proverbs 15

## Verses (33)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | By a soft answer wrath is turned away, but a bitter word is a cause of angry feelings. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Knowledge is dropping from the tongue of the wise; but from the mouth of the foolish comes a stream of foolish words. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | The eyes of the Lord are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | A comforting tongue is a tree of life, but a twisted tongue is a crushing of the spirit. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | A foolish man puts no value on his father's training; but he who has respect for teaching has good sense. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | In the house of the upright man there is a great store of wealth; but in the profits of the sinner there is trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | The lips of the wise keep knowledge, but the heart of the foolish man is not right. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | The offering of the evil-doer is disgusting to the Lord, but the prayer of the upright man is his delight. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | The way of the evil-doer is disgusting to the Lord, but he who goes after righteousness is dear to him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | There is bitter punishment for him who is turned from the way; and death will be the fate of the hater of teaching. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Before the Lord are the underworld and destruction: how much more, then, the hearts of the children of men! | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | The hater of authority has no love for teaching: he will not go to the wise. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | A glad heart makes a shining face, but by the sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | The heart of the man of good sense goes in search of knowledge, but foolish things are the food of the unwise. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | All the days of the troubled are evil; but he whose heart is glad has an unending feast. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | Better is a little with the fear of the Lord, than great wealth together with trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Better is a simple meal where love is, than a fat ox and hate with it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | An angry man makes men come to blows, but he who is slow to get angry puts an end to fighting. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Thorns are round the way of the hater of work; but the road of the hard worker becomes a highway. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish man has no respect for his mother. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | Foolish behaviour is joy to the unwise; but a man of good sense makes his way straight. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Where there are no wise suggestions, purposes come to nothing; but by a number of wise guides they are made certain. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | A man has joy in the answer of his mouth: and a word at the right time, how good it is! | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Acting wisely is the way of life, guiding a man away from the underworld. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | The house of the man of pride will be uprooted by the Lord, but he will make safe the heritage of the widow. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | Evil designs are disgusting to the Lord, but the words of the clean-hearted are pleasing. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | He whose desires are fixed on profit is a cause of trouble to his family; but he who has no desire for offerings will have life. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | The heart of the upright gives thought to his answer; but from the mouth of the evil-doer comes a stream of evil things. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | The Lord is far from sinners, but his ear is open to the prayer of the upright. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | The light of the eyes is a joy to the heart, and good news makes the bones fat. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | The man whose ear is open to the teaching of life will have his place among the wise. | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
| [32](BBE.md#v32) | He who will not be controlled by training has no respect for his soul, but he who gives ear to teaching will get wisdom. | [WEB](WEB.md#v32) · [KJV](KJV.md#v32) · [ASV](ASV.md#v32) |
| [33](BBE.md#v33) | The fear of the Lord is the teaching of wisdom; and a low opinion of oneself goes before honour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v33) · [KJV](KJV.md#v33) · [ASV](ASV.md#v33) |
